description: Phase 1, PRD 1 of 2 of the coherence refactoring. Extracts a TaskLifecycle service at src/lifecycle/ that owns all tasks.status mutations + side effects (run_tasks insert, PRD JSON sync, decay columns, notes formatting, stderr warning shape). Replaces ~20 raw `UPDATE tasks SET status` sites across 13+ files with a single named surface (six verbs: apply, try_claim, recover_in_progress_for_prefix, auto_block_after_failures, resurrect_for_iteration, reconcile_from_prd, repair_stale). Vertical-slice migration (skip.rs first, with M=3 mini-dogfood gate) precedes the bulk migration. Strict ordering vs the parallel runner-trait-hygiene effort enforced via CLARIFY-001.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output for affected crate
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` for affected crate
Rust: Scoped `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>` passes for the touched module
No raw `UPDATE tasks SET status` SQL added to production code (only one exception in commands/init/mod.rs is permitted, marked LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION)
No new `.unwrap()` in production paths; use `?` propagation
All status-write call sites that previously called raw SQL now call a TaskLifecycle verb (or are explicitly out-of-scope per LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION lint)

The five contract-level invariants (auto-claim, per-task partial-failure tolerance, DB-authoritative-PRD-best-effort, exact stderr warning shape, conditional-WHERE in API) are preserved bit-identically
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes and the full-dogfood gate (VERIFY-001) is signed off. Squash optional. PRD 2 (Engine Orchestration Boundaries) is authored only after this PRD merges and the dogfood gate is satisfied.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding; this refactor touches live-dogfooded code
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Stable lifecycle seam unblocks Phase 1 PRD 2 (engine carve) and Phase 2 prompt assembler; 1:10+ savings ratio justified
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan; preserve all five contract-level invariants
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically; shadow tests assert byte-identical DB diff
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings, no .unwrap() in production
6. POLISH — Docs (src/lifecycle/CLAUDE.md, project CLAUDE.md pointer), formatting
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — every task carries qualityDimensions for THIS refactor's contract-preservation focus
Phase 2 foundation — the lifecycle seam is the prerequisite for PRD 2; over-investing in correctness here is correct
Edge cases = test cases — every named edge case (auto-claim, partial failure, etc.) becomes a TEST-INIT entry
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones; milestones must leave the trunk green
Shadow tests are the safety net — a site is migrated only when its shadow test is green
Per-task partial-failure tolerance in apply() is a HARD contract — never convert to batch-level Result<(), Err>

prohibitedOutcomes: Converting apply() to return a single Result<(), Err> at the batch level (per-task outcomes are a contract; learning #2284)
Hiding the conditional-WHERE expected-status set behind an unconditional try_claim method (must remain explicit per FR-005)
Adding a NEW transaction wrapper around code that previously ran outside one (changes observable failure semantics)
Adding SELECT-before-UPDATE round-trips where today's SQL writes via a conditional WHERE
Reformatting the 'PRD JSON sync failed for {task}: {err}' stderr line (operators grep for this exact prefix)
Folding doctor/fixes.rs into reconcile_from_prd (must remain a distinct repair_stale verb per §6 doctor sub-decision)
Editing tasks/tasklifecycle-extraction.json directly — use task-mgr CLI
Touching the five parallel-slot cascade defenses (synthetic shared-infra slot, buildy-prefix heuristic, ephemeral overlay, consecutive-merge-fail halt, stale-ephemeral hygiene)
Removing the LIFECYCLE-EXCEPTION comment from commands/init/mod.rs:517 (lint-enforced)
Bulk migration starting before CLARIFY-002 (mini-dogfood gate) passes
Renaming TaskStatus enum variants or changing on-disk task status strings (DB migration impact; orthogonal to refactor goal — PRD §5 Non-Goal)
Changing the <task-status> side-band tag wire format or recognized status set (the tag contract is stable across both this PRD and the runner-trait-hygiene PRD — PRD §5 Non-Goal)

userStories.description: Write exhaustive unit test asserting the (from, to, source) triple acceptance matrix for the future TaskLifecycle validator. Tests live initially in src/models/task.rs (alongside existing matrix tests at lines 513-571) and will move with the matrix to src/lifecycle/matrix.rs in FEAT-002. The triple shape is load-bearing: TransitionSource::ReconcilePrd is allowed to flip terminal states that TransitionSource::Operator cannot. A (from, to)-only test would let regressions land green.
edgeCases: (from=Todo, to=Done, source=Operator) — REJECTED today (must claim first), but ACCEPTED through apply() auto-claim path (preserved as service-internal behavior, not a matrix change)
(from=Done, to=Irrelevant, source=Operator) — REJECTED (terminal → terminal disallowed for operator intent)
(from=Done, to=Irrelevant, source=ReconcilePrd) — ACCEPTED (prd_reconcile.rs:550 deliberately flips terminal states)
(from=InProgress, to=Todo, source=DoctorRepair) — ACCEPTED (doctor/fixes.rs:30 stale recovery)
(from=Blocked, to=InProgress, source=any) — REJECTED (must go Blocked → Todo → InProgress; unblock.rs path)
(from=Skipped, to=Todo, source=Recovery) — ACCEPTED (resurrect_for_iteration)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test fails to compile — table-driven design requires the third axis

Every (from, to) pair has at least one source that permits it OR every source rejects it (no orphaned rejection-only pairs without rationale)
userStories.notes: Per FR-002. Use a table-driven test (Vec<(from, to, source, expected)>) so failures pinpoint the regressing triple. Existing matrix tests in task.rs:513-571 are the Operator-source baseline.
qualityDimensions: Each case must have a clear expected-outcome rationale (link to PRD section or existing matrix)

userStories.description: Implement `TaskLifecycle::try_claim(task_id: &str, allowed: &[TaskStatus]) -> Result<bool>` in src/lifecycle/claim.rs (per FR-005).
